name: Parcel Size Limits (length + girth)
description: Carrier maximum-size limits expressed as length and length-plus-girth, where girth = 2 x (width + height). UPS and Australia Post rows are confirmed against carrier pages (checked 2026-07-02); remaining rows are candidates that MUST be confirmed against current carrier guides.
source: UPS 'Shipping Dimensions and Weight' page and Australia Post 'Size and Weight Guidelines' page, both checked 2026-07-02 via corpus-logistics-supply-chain/sourced-reference-data-2026-07-02.md. FedEx and USPS rows: TO CONFIRM against current service guides.
sourcing_notes: Define girth = 2*(width + height); length-plus-girth = length + girth. UPS US small package max confirmed: 108 in length, 165 in length+girth, 150 lb (UPS official page, checked 2026-07-02). Australia Post international parcels confirmed: 105 cm max length, 140 cm max girth (girth alone, not length+girth - AusPost uses girth = (width + height) x 2), 20 kg (AusPost guidelines, checked 2026-07-02). FedEx Ground commonly cited at 108 in / 165 in but not confirmed by the 2026-07-02 pack; USPS retail ground 130 in length+girth unconfirmed. Table status stays needs_sourcing until all rows are verified. Record effective_date per carrier once fully confirmed.
